source: CIS AWS STORAGE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1
title: Ensure to create IAM roles for Backup

input_row.source: CIS AWS STORAGE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1
input_row.title: Ensure to create IAM roles for Backup
input_row.description: An AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) role is similar to a user, in that it is an AWS identity with permissions policies that determine what the identity can and cannot do in AWS. However, instead of being uniquely associated with one person, a role is intended to be assumable by anyone who needs it.
input_row.rationale: While Service Linked Roles offer quick deployment, using default configurations isn't recommended for security best practices.
input_row.audit: To create a role for AWS Backup, follow these steps: 1. Navigate to the "IAM Dashboard" in the AWS Console. 2. Select "Roles" from the left-hand menu. 3. Click on the "Create Role" button. 4. Choose "AWS Service" as the trusted entity. 5. Select "AWS Backup" as the service that will use this role. 6. Choose a policy to apply to the role or create a custom policy. 7. Review the role details and provide a meaningful name for the role. 8. Click on "Create Role" to finalize the creation of the role for AWS Backup.
input_row.remediation: Assess your organization's needs to determine whether to utilize Service Linked Roles for AWS backups. Default Value: When using the AWS Backup console for the first time, you can choose to have AWS Backup create a default service role for you. This role has the permissions that AWS Backup needs to create and restore backups on your behalf. References: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/using-service-linked- roles.html

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

- ✓ Can AWS APIs query this property? → Automated
- ✓ Is this a standard AWS service property (encryption, logging, access control, network rules)? → Automated
- ✓ Does boto3 SDK have methods to check this? → Automated
- ✓ Does AWS CLI have commands to retrieve this? → Automated

The control is about ensuring an IAM role is set up for AWS Backup, which is a standard AWS service property related to access control. IAM roles are accessible via AWS APIs, boto3 SDK, and AWS CLI, which makes this an objective and automatable check.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_iam_role_aws_backup_service_linked_present

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- **AWS API calls or boto3 SDK methods to use**: Use `boto3` with the IAM client method `list_roles()` to enumerate current IAM roles.
- **AWS CLI commands (if applicable)**: Use `aws iam list-roles` to list all IAM roles in the account.
- **Specific resources to enumerate**: Enumerate IAM roles across the account.
- **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate**: Check for the existence of a role with the necessary permissions for AWS Backup, usually this will be a service-linked role `AWSBackupServiceRolePolicyForBackup`.
- **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values**: Pass if there exists at least one role specifically linked to AWS Backup operations, usually named similar to `AWSBackupDefaultServiceRole` or appropriately configured with AWS Backup permissions. Fail if no such role exists.

By automating this verification, organizations can quickly ascertain whether the proper AWS Backup IAM roles have been configured, in line with the compliance requirement.
